Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Lucas covers local community issues in the Washington, DC area such as transportation, housing and education as well as public safety. His coverage area includes Northern Virginia and suburban Maryland. Send him info on local issues, guests or non-profit organizations. "Honestly, I don't care if someone wrote a book," he says. "It doesn't mean a thing relating to what I cover." He hosts an hour-long Sunday morning public affairs program. "There are 2-4 interviews per show, almost exclusively local guests discussing local issues," he says. Contact by email.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Brooks is a co-host on the station's morning show, "Loo and Lori" and hosts "Women of Vision," a 25-minute public affairs program. For her public affairs interview show, pitch her female guests "who are passionate about what they are saying, not someone who is a media person for the company for whom it's just a job," she says. Any pitches for male guests get deleted. "I would prefer that if they're going to send me something they suggest some ideas," she says. "Like Shirley is available for a phone interview or an in-studio interview next Thursday morning." The best PR pros "work with me and my request for 25 minutes instead of ten minutes," she says. Send press releases for info on people and services as well as local events, but not meetings. Contact by email.
